conditional_small_noise_closure.directional_gain_condition: L_sigma=O(sigma^-gamma) uniformly over the two contours and all dyadic refinements
conditional_small_noise_closure.polylogarithmic_gain_case: ||I_n,sigma||_S2=O(n^-2 sigma^-3 polylog(1/sigma)) and the defect is lower order than n^-1 sigma^-3/2

directional_bound.bound: length(Gamma)/(2 pi) max_Gamma|z| sup_Gamma||R_D(z)|| times the product of the two directional quantities
directional_bound.symmetric_alternative: interchange the Hilbert-Schmidt and operator norms
directional_bound.why_weaker_than_global: only resolvent action on the two coupling ranges is charged

evidence_level.analytic: exact Hilbert-space Schur identity, Hilbert-Schmidt directional bound, dyadic telescoping theorem, and conditional small-noise power theorem
evidence_level.numerical: binary64 sparse eigendata on exact Haar-compressed finite matrices; not interval validated

exact_schur_identification.identity: E_n Q_Gamma(K) E_n-Q_Gamma(A)=(2 pi i)^-1 int_Gamma z S_n(z) B R_D(z) C (z-A)^-1 dz
exact_schur_identification.structural_order: quadratic in the coarse-detail couplings B and C

limitations: The exact Schur identity and dyadic telescoping theorem are unconditional operator statements, but their uniform small-noise application requires the displayed directional gain condition.
No global or directional reduced L2 resolvent upper is proved for sigma tending to zero.
The observed n^-2 sigma^-2 law is a binary64 finite-matrix fit and is not promoted to an analytic theorem.
The sparse audit uses an eight-sigma cutoff, exact row renormalization, and floating eigensolvers; it is not an interval enclosure.
The theorem concerns intrinsic peripheral identification and bulk-square approximation, not an arithmetic trace formula or prime-power identity.
No zeta-zero identification, self-adjoint Hilbert-Polya operator, T log T counting law, or Riemann-hypothesis conclusion is claimed.

residue_reduced_split.coarse_residue_growth_required_for_corol: O(sqrt(log(1/sigma))) uniformly over dyadic levels; not proved here
residue_reduced_split.formula: R(z)=Pi/(z-lambda)+R_circ(z), applied separately to the fine top-left and coarse resolvents
residue_reduced_split.uniform_reduced_directional_consequence: directional gain O(log(1/sigma)) after multiplying the two sides

scope: intrinsic Perron-plus-parity weighted-Riesz identification for nested cell-average Galerkin compressions of the folded-Gaussian operator
